questions.question: 2H 2 +  O 2 →  2H 2 O Which statement about the reactions is not correct? A In reaction 1, copper( II) oxide is reduced to copper., B In reaction 2, carbon monoxide is oxidised to carbon dioxide., C In reactions 1 and 3, hydrogen is oxidised to water., D In reaction 2, iron( III) oxide is oxidised to iron. 8
questions.text: 2H 2 +  O 2 →  2H 2 O Which statement about the reactions is not correct? A In reaction 1, copper( II) oxide is reduced to copper., B In reaction 2, carbon monoxide is oxidised to carbon dioxide., C In reactions 1 and 3, hydrogen is oxidised to water., D In reaction 2, iron( III) oxide is oxidised to iron. 8
options: A In reaction 1, copper( II) oxide is reduced to copper.
B In reaction 2, carbon monoxide is oxidised to carbon dioxide.
C In reactions 1 and 3, hydrogen is oxidised to water.
D In reaction 2, iron( III) oxide is oxidised to iron. 8

questions.question: Compound T is added to dilute hydrochloric acid and warmed gently. The mixture gives off a gas which turns acidified aqueous potassium manganate( VII) from purple to colourless. A flame test on compound T gives a lilac flame. What is compound T?, A sodium sulfate, B sodium sulfite, C potassium sulfate, D potassium sulfite

A flame test on compound T gives a lilac flame. What is compound T?, A sodium sulfate, B sodium sulfite, C potassium sulfate, D potassium sulfite

questions.question: Elements P and Q are in the same period of the Periodic Table. P is a metal and Q is a non-metal. Which statement is correct? A P has a greater nucleon number than Q., B P is to the right of Q in the period., C Q has more electron shells than P., D Q has more protons than P.
questions.text: Elements P and Q are in the same period of the Periodic Table. P is a metal and Q is a non-metal. Which statement is correct? A P has a greater nucleon number than Q., B P is to the right of Q in the period., C Q has more electron shells than P., D Q has more protons than P.
options: A P has a greater nucleon number than Q.
B P is to the right of Q in the period.
C Q has more electron shells than P.
D Q has more protons than P.

questions.question: What   is   not a property of Group I metals? A They are soft and can be cut with a knife., B They react when exposed to oxygen in the air., C They produce an acidic solution when they react with water., D They react rapidly with water producing hydrogen gas. 10
questions.text: What   is   not a property of Group I metals? A They are soft and can be cut with a knife., B They react when exposed to oxygen in the air., C They produce an acidic solution when they react with water., D They react rapidly with water producing hydrogen gas. 10
options: A They are soft and can be cut with a knife.
B They react when exposed to oxygen in the air.
C They produce an acidic solution when they react with water.
D They react rapidly with water producing hydrogen gas. 10

questions.question: Which statement about the element bromine is correct? A It displaces chlorine from aqueous potassium chloride., B It has a higher density than chlorine., C It is a diatomic metal., D It is a green gas at room temperature.
questions.text: Which statement about the element bromine is correct? A It displaces chlorine from aqueous potassium chloride., B It has a higher density than chlorine., C It is a diatomic metal., D It is a green gas at room temperature.
options: A It displaces chlorine from aqueous potassium chloride.
B It has a higher density than chlorine.
C It is a diatomic metal.
D It is a green gas at room temperature.

questions.question: Four metals are listed in decreasing order of reactivity. magnesium zinc iron copper Titanium reacts with acid and cannot be extracted from its ore by heating with carbon. Where should titanium be placed in the list? A below copper, B between iron and copper, C between magnesium and zinc, D between zinc and iron 11
questions.text: Four metals are listed in decreasing order of reactivity. magnesium zinc iron copper Titanium reacts with acid and cannot be extracted from its ore by heating with carbon. Where should titanium be placed in the list? A below copper, B between iron and copper, C between magnesium and zinc, D between zinc and iron 11

questions.question: Basic oxides and oxygen are used to convert iron into steel. Which statement is not correct? A Carbon is converted into carbon dioxide., B Silicon is converted into silicon( IV) oxide., C The basic oxides react with acidic impurities to form slag., D The oxygen reacts with the iron to produce hematite.
questions.text: Basic oxides and oxygen are used to convert iron into steel. Which statement is not correct? A Carbon is converted into carbon dioxide., B Silicon is converted into silicon( IV) oxide., C The basic oxides react with acidic impurities to form slag., D The oxygen reacts with the iron to produce hematite.
options: A Carbon is converted into carbon dioxide.
B Silicon is converted into silicon( IV) oxide.
C The basic oxides react with acidic impurities to form slag.
D The oxygen reacts with the iron to produce hematite.

questions.question: A student added dilute hydrochloric acid to four metals and recorded the results. Some of the results are not correct. results metal gas given off 1            copper            yes 2               iron               yes 3        magnesium        no 4              zinc              yes Which two results are correct? A 1 and 3 B    1 and 4 C    2 and 3 D    2 and 4
questions.text: A student added dilute hydrochloric acid to four metals and recorded the results. Some of the results are not correct. results metal gas given off 1            copper            yes 2               iron               yes 3        magnesium        no 4              zinc              yes Which two results are correct? A 1 and 3 B    1 and 4 C    2 and 3 D    2 and 4
options: A 1 and 3 B    1 and 4 C    2 and 3 D    2 and 4

questions.question: Some properties of aluminium are listed. 1     It     conducts     heat. 2     It has a low density. 3     It is mechanically strong. 4     It is resistant to corrosion. questions.text: Some properties of aluminium are listed. 1     It     conducts     heat. 2     It has a low density. 3     It is mechanically strong. 4     It is resistant to corrosion. Which properties make aluminium suitable for making food containers for chilled food products? A 1, 2 and 4 B    1, 3 and 4 C    1    only    D    4    only 12
options: A 1, 2 and 4 B    1, 3 and 4 C    1    only    D    4    only 12

questions.question: Calcium oxide and ammonium salts are used by farmers to treat soils. Why are these two substances added at different times? A They are both acidic., B They are both basic., C They react with each other to produce ammonia., D They react with each other to produce hydrogen.
questions.text: Calcium oxide and ammonium salts are used by farmers to treat soils. Why are these two substances added at different times? A They are both acidic., B They are both basic., C They react with each other to produce ammonia., D They react with each other to produce hydrogen.
options: C They react with each other to produce ammonia.
D They react with each other to produce hydrogen.

questions.question: Which  list  shows  the  fractions  obtained  from  distilling  petroleum,  in  order  of  increasing  boiling point? A bitumen    → diesel oil → fuel oil → lubricating oil, B diesel    oil    → gasoline → naphtha → kerosene, C gasoline    → naphtha → kerosene → diesel oil, D kerosene    → lubricating oil → naphtha → refinery gas
questions.text: Which  list  shows  the  fractions  obtained  from  distilling  petroleum,  in  order  of  increasing  boiling point? A bitumen    → diesel oil → fuel oil → lubricating oil, B diesel    oil    → gasoline → naphtha → kerosene, C gasoline    → naphtha → kerosene → diesel oil, D kerosene    → lubricating oil → naphtha → refinery gas
options: A bitumen    → diesel oil → fuel oil → lubricating oil
B diesel    oil    → gasoline → naphtha → kerosene
C gasoline    → naphtha → kerosene → diesel oil
D kerosene    → lubricating oil → naphtha → refinery gas
